feed.title: HealthRankings — Health News
feed.subtitle: Latest US health headlines. Full list: https://healthrankings.co/healthrankings-news.html

entry.title: Possible culprit found for the ‘spoonful of microplastics’ in our brains
entry.summary: A new study may have found the culprit behind microplastic accumulation in our brains, and it’s much more common than we may realize.

entry.title: The number of steps you need to walk a day to maintain weight loss — no, it’s not 10,000
entry.summary: While 10,000 steps is sold as the magic number to achieve weight loss, science says we should aim for different digits.

entry.title: A Mother's Brain Is Profoundly Rewired by Pregnancy, Scans Show
entry.summary: Up until the 1970s or so, the human brain was assumed to be 'hardwired' like a computer, its neural networks remaining fixed throughout adulthood.

entry.title: Hantavirus live updates: Evacuations begin after MV Hondius arrives in Canary Islands
entry.summary: Teams will bring back Americans to the National Quarantine Unit in Nebraska.

entry.title: FDA claims there’s no estrogen patch shortage as women struggle to get prescriptions filled
entry.summary: The Food and Drug Administration removed the black box warning from hormone replacement therapies late last year, and recently, the most insured type, the estrogen patch, has been in short supply amid a boom in the therapy’s popularity.

entry.title: My Mom Has Always Seemed Unenthused About My Twins. But Now She’s Been Making Uncomfortable Comments.
entry.summary: She has to stop!

entry.title: Versions of You in Other Universes May Be Subtly Affecting Your Destiny, Oxford Physicist Says
entry.summary: Oxford Physicist Vlatko Vedral argues that observing quantum objects affect us, not the other way around, for better or worse.

entry.title: Psychologists Say People Who Still Use Paper Calendars Aren't Stubborn or Old-Fashioned. Their Brains Are Wired to Process Information in a Richer Way
entry.summary: Handwriting on paper doesn't just feel different. It forces the brain into a deeper kind of encoding that screens cannot trigger, and the difference shows up on MRI scans within an hour.

entry.title: Indian Techie, 37, Dies In California After Battling Valley Fever For A Month
entry.summary: A GoFundMe page started by his cousin, Rama Koteswara Rao, said Chiranjeevi first fell ill in April with symptoms that appeared similar to a common flu.

entry.title: French passenger of hantavirus cruise ship starts showing symptoms on evacuation flight, prime minister says
entry.summary: A Dutch-flagged cruise ship that was hit by a deadly hantavirus outbreak reached Spain's Canary Islands early Sunday morning.

entry.title: Magnetic Brain Pulses Help Kids With Autism to Communicate, Study Finds
entry.summary: For children with autism spectrum disorder and with an intellectual disability, the options for improving communication and social skills are limited.

entry.title: Arizona woman's tattoo lands her in hospital with severe complications
entry.summary: Stephanie's dream tattoo turned into a nightmare, landing her in the hospital with complications. She also discovered unexpected news amid the ordeal.

entry.title: Outbreaks Of Highly Contagious Disease Reach Record High In CA
entry.summary: The rate of infections in California continues to outpace national trends, affecting more native-born residents than in the past.

entry.title: New Pill Lowers Stubborn Blood Pressure and Protects the Kidneys
entry.summary: A new experimental drug lowered blood pressure and sharply reduced signs of kidney damage in people with chronic kidney disease.

entry.title: 115 sick in norovirus outbreak on Florida-bound cruise ship, CDC says
entry.summary: More than 100 people have fallen ill with norovirus on a cruise ship that left Florida last week and is set to return soon, the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention said.

entry.title: Norovirus outbreak reported on Princess cruise ship that departed Fort Lauderdale
entry.summary: This is the second time within two months that a norovirus outbreak happened on a Princess ship based out of the Fort Lauderdale.

entry.title: Hantavirus Cruise Passengers Will Be Evacuated Soon, Report Says (Latest Updates)
entry.summary: There are nine U.S. residents being monitored for the illness across six states, though none have shown any signs of illness.

entry.title: Why this tick season could be the worst in a decade
entry.summary: During the fourth week of April, about 114 out of every 100,000 emergency department visits nationwide were for tick bites.

entry.title: The surprisingly strong case for feeling great about your coffee habit
entry.summary: Coffee was once treated as a health risk. Newer research links moderate drinking to lower risks for dementia, diabetes, liver disease, and early death.

entry.title: Scientists discover a hydraulic link between the abdomen and the brain
entry.summary: A new study suggests that flexing your abdominal muscles physically moves your brain. This gentle shifting acts as a hydraulic pump, forcing fluids through brain tissue to wash away harmful waste and promote cognitive health.

entry.title: YouTuber Catches Heat After Attending Wedding Amid Hantavirus Outbreak
entry.summary: If you attended a recent wedding with YouTuber Ruhi Çenet in Istanbul, then you may want to get checked for the deadly hantavirus before it's too late!

entry.title: Common stroke may actually be caused by widening of brain arteries
entry.summary: The widening of arteries within the brain was associated with both lacunar stroke and cerebral small vessel disease in a new study, casting doubts over previous understanding of stroke causes.

entry.title: VIDEO: Texas mom shares daughter's hantavirus story as cruise ship outbreak draws attention
entry.summary: A Texas mom is sharing how her healthy 27-year-old daughter died in 2018 after contracting hantavirus. She hopes her daughter's story will raise awareness and that it encourages more research into hantavirus.

entry.title: Health Officials Warn Of Potential Measles Exposure At Roslyn Heights Restaurant
entry.summary: A laboratory verified a measles case in a New York City resident, who potentially exposed the highly contagious disease to patrons who visited the 388 Italian Restaurant in Roslyn Heights on May 3.

entry.title: Arizona man who survived hantavirus 24 years ago, but lost mom and sister, says recent outbreak is "hard" to process
entry.summary: In 2002, Zermeño found out he contracted hantavirus after cleaning the family house following the death of his mother and sister. He had been exposed to rodent droppings and became infected.

entry.title: Texas doctor reignites ivermectin debate with new hantavirus claims
entry.summary: Houston doctor Mary Bowden is offering ivermectin to Texans years after gaining attention during the COVID-19 pandemic.

entry.title: What Are You Actually Swallowing When You Take Probiotics?
entry.summary: Standing by the counter at the pharmacist waiting to pick up my prescription, I couldn't help noticing the prominent display of probiotics on the counter.

entry.title: Why health officials say the hantavirus cruise outbreak is not the next pandemic
entry.summary: Experts say that because this strain of hantavirus typically requires prolonged or intimate contact, it is not as contagious as covid during the pandemic.

entry.title: 115 people sickened in norovirus outbreak on cruise ship: CDC
entry.summary: According to the CDC, 102 passengers and 13 crew were reported sick.

entry.title: “Sounds Like 2026”: Disturbing 'The X-Files' Moment Resurfaces as Hantavirus Spreads in US
entry.summary: Fans notice eerie real-world similarity in viral The X-Files scene amid hantavirus outbreak in the US.

entry.title: Moderna (MRNA) Stock Surges 14% on Hantavirus Vaccine. Here’s What Investors Should Know
entry.summary: Moderna ($MRNA) shares are surging 14% at the time of writing as investors react to the hantavirus and the biotech company’s early vaccine work. The virus has been back in

entry.title: YouTuber went to packed wedding after leaving MV Hondius as hantavirus spread: ‘We were not well informed’
entry.summary: “We were not well informed,” Cenet said.

entry.title: Is it true that eating more eggs could help lower Alzheimer's risk?
entry.summary: People who eat eggs at least five times a week appear to have a lower risk of Alzheimer's disease but experts caution that there are more factors to take into account.

entry.title: Alzheimer’s study finds link between breakfast staple and reduced risk of disease
entry.summary: A study from researchers at Loma Linda University in California suggests a specific nutrient-rich food — fairly frequently — could be linked to lower risk of Alzheimer’s disease among older adults.

entry.title: I’m an ICU Doctor Who Treated a Critically Ill Hantavirus Patient. Here’s What Everyone Should Know.
entry.summary: An ICU physician who treated a hantavirus patient explains how the virus spreads, why it can become deadly so quickly, and when to seek emergency medical care.

entry.title: New Suspected Hantavirus Case Reported In Spain (Live Updates)
entry.summary: The MV Hondius is en route to the Canary Islands, where it will be allowed to dock for the first time since a hantavirus outbreak was confirmed on board.

entry.title: Metformin lowers blood glucose by targeting intestinal mitochondrial complex I
entry.summary: Metabolomic analysis of plasma from metformin-treated humans, together with mechanistic studies in mouse models, reveals that metformin selectively inhibits mitochondrial complex I in the intestinal epithelium. This mechanism unifies multiple clinical effects…

entry.title: As federal government pulls back, Colorado charts independent course with vaccines
entry.summary: As state leaders change laws to make vaccines more accessible, a coalition of doctors, public health advocates and everyday Coloradans is trying to start a public conversation about their importance.

entry.title: Nearly 3,000 peer-reviewed medical papers have fake citations, AI-assisted audit finds
entry.summary: A new Columbia University School of Nursing AI-assisted audit reveals nearly 3,000 peer-reviewed medical papers have fake citations that do not exist in scientific databases. The results highlight an alarming trend in academic publishing as the use of AI grow…

entry.title: Babies are bleeding to death as parents reject a vitamin shot given at birth
entry.summary: Vitamin K shots, which help the blood to clot, are one of three key interventions for newborns, along with an antibiotic eye ointment and the hepatitis B vaccine.

entry.title: Surge in fake citations uncovered by audit of 2.5 million biomedical science papers
entry.summary: An analysis of 97 million citations has found that rates of fabricated citations have climbed steeply since 2023.

entry.title: Hantavirus latest updates: U.S. among countries searching for passengers who left Dutch cruise ship after 1st death
entry.summary: Three people — a Dutch couple and a German national — have died in the outbreak on the MV Hondius. There are eight confirmed or suspected cases in total.

entry.title: 2 Hantavirus Cruise Passengers Are Texas Residents (Live Updates)
entry.summary: The MV Hondius is en route to the Canary Islands, where it will be allowed to dock for the first time since a hantavirus outbreak was confirmed on board.

entry.title: Infectious disease experts alarmed by Legionella infections at Bay Area hospital
entry.summary: On Monday, Kaiser Permanente Santa Clara Medical Center said it had recently confirmed 18 cases of legionella infections at the hospital.

entry.title: Eating eggs could cut Alzheimer’s risk by 27%
entry.summary: Eating eggs might do more than just start your day—it could help protect your brain. Researchers found that people 65 and older who eat eggs regularly have a significantly lower risk of developing Alzheimer’s disease, with daily or near-daily consumption link…

entry.title: As measles roars back, scientists find antibodies that could offer protection
entry.summary: Scientists just took a step toward developing a therapy that could prevent someone from getting measles after they have been exposed.

entry.title: Met Opera patrons warned of measles exposure after La Bohème matinee
entry.summary: City officials said the infected person is an unvaccinated adult, who tested positive after international travel.

entry.title: Health officials track dozens who left hantavirus-stricken ship after 1st fatality
entry.summary: Oceanwide Expeditions has revealed that 30 passengers disembarked from its cruise ship hit by a hantavirus outbreak on April 24, on the remote island of St. Helena, nearly two weeks after the first passenger died.

entry.title: Woman, 48, Dies After Grueling Day of Plastic Surgery Procedures: Lawsuit
entry.summary: “She saw herself, I think, coming out of the surgery with the body she wanted,” her widower said.

entry.title: There is no vaccine for deadly hantavirus: what that means for future outbreaks
entry.summary: Virologist Jay Hooper is developing a vaccine for the rare rodent virus behind an outbreak on a cruise ship.
